metadata.note: All single-layer tests. Multi-layer combinations (e.g. trans + running-key) are in a separate underdetermined category.

eliminated.evidence: Script e_disproof_01_caesar_all_shifts.py tested all 25 shifts exhaustively. Best result: ROT2 → 3/24 crib matches. BERLINCLOCK does not appear at positions 63-73 for ANY shift. EASTNORTHEAST does not appear at positions 21-33 for ANY shift. BERLINCLOCK does not appear ANYWHERE in any shifted output. Verdict line: [DERIVED FACT] Simple Caesar cipher (ROT-1 through ROT-25): ELIMINATED.

Script e_affine_mono_disproof.py proved TWO ways: (1) ALGEBRAIC: Intersection of all 24 crib constraints (b = CT[pos] - a*PT[pos] mod 26) yields EMPTY SET — no (a,b) pair satisfies all cribs simultaneously. Example contradiction: pos 24 (CT=V, PT=T) and pos 22 (CT=L, PT=A) require a*11≡10 mod 26, which has no solution in VALID_A. (2) EXHAUSTIVE: Tested all 312 keys; best 5/24 crib score. Bean equality (PT[27]=PT[65]) is automatically satisfied since CT[27]=CT[65]=P, but this is irrelevant given zero keys satisfy all cribs.

eliminated.cipher: Simple Monoalphabetic Substitution (any bijection over 26 letters)
eliminated.evidence: Direct algebraic proof from crib positions. A monoalphabetic substitution requires each CT letter to map to exactly one PT letter (bijection). From the 24 known crib positions (EASTNORTHEAST at 21-33, BERLINCLOCK at 63-73), 9 contradictions are provable: Q→N (pos 25) vs Q→O (pos 26); R→S (pos 23) vs R→T (pos 28); S→S (pos 32) vs S→T (pos 33); N→H (pos 29) vs N→B (pos 63); V→T (pos 24) vs V→L (pos 66); T→I (pos 67) vs T→N (pos 68); F→E (pos 21) vs F→O (pos 71); P→R (pos 27) vs P→C (pos 72); K→A (pos 31) vs K→K (pos 73). Nine of 14 CT letters at crib positions require two different PT letters. No bijection can satisfy this. This proof is key-independent: no alphabetic permutation can fix it.

TWO independent structural proofs: (1) PARITY: Playfair encrypts digraphs → output length is always even. K4 CT length = 97 (odd prime). No Playfair key, keyword, or padding scheme can produce odd-length CT. (2) ALPHABET: Playfair uses a 5×5 grid = 25-letter alphabet (I and J share one cell). K4 CT contains both I (4×: positions 16,56,59,84) and J (3×: positions 40,51,81), and uses all 26 distinct letters. A 25-letter cipher cannot produce 26 distinct output letters. Both proofs are mathematical certainties. Hill-climbing (20 restarts × 5000 iterations) on CT[:96] confirms: best Playfair key achieves noise-level quadgram score and 0 crib matches. Script: e_playfair_01_full_disproof.py, also E-FRAC-21 Proof 8.

K4 CT uses all 26 distinct letters. Bifid 5×5 uses a 5×5 = 25-letter Polybius square with I/J merged into one cell. Output alphabet therefore has ≤25 letters. K4 CT contains both I (4 occurrences) and J (3 occurrences) as separate distinct letters. Contradiction: a 25-letter output alphabet cannot produce 26 distinct CT letters. E-FRAC-21 Proof 4.

STATISTICAL: From E-FRAC-13, Bifid 6×6 applied to English plaintext produces IC ≈ 0.059-0.069. K4 IC = 0.036, which falls at the 0th percentile of Bifid 6×6 output distributions. IC is invariant under transposition, so Bifid 6×6 + any transposition also has IC ≈ 0.059-0.069. K4 IC is incompatible (p < 0.001). PARTIAL ALGEBRAIC (E-S-09): Of 48 periods tested (2-49), 10 are algebraically eliminated by direct contradiction (same grid cell forced to two different CT values). 38 periods survive due to insufficient crib-position overlap within blocks, not due to consistency. CAVEAT: Under the masking hypothesis (Scheidt 2005 'I masked the English language'), IC-based arguments are weakened since the masked PT may not be English-distributed. This elevates Bifid 6×6 from 'statistically eliminated' to 'highly unlikely but underdetermined under masking'. E-FRAC-21 Proof 5.

Four-Square is a digraph cipher using two 5×5 grids (I/J merged, 25-letter alphabet each). TWO independent structural proofs: (1) PARITY: Each plaintext digraph maps to one ciphertext digraph → output always even-length. K4 CT = 97 chars (odd prime). Impossible. (2) ALPHABET: 5×5 grid = 25 letters. K4 CT uses 26 distinct letters. Contradiction. Same proofs apply to Two-Square. E-FRAC-21 Proof 9.

ADFGVX is a fractionation cipher: each plaintext letter is converted to a bigram (2 CT letters) from the set {A,D,F,G,V,X}, then the sequence of bigrams is transposed. The output length is always 2 × plaintext_length = even. K4 CT length = 97 (odd prime). No ADFGVX key, grid, or transposition scheme can produce 97 output characters. Mathematical impossibility. E-FRAC-21 Proof 1.

Same parity argument as ADFGVX. ADFGX uses a 5×5 Polybius square with 5 fractionation letters {A,D,F,G,X}. Each PT letter → 2-letter bigram → output always even-length. K4 CT = 97 (odd). Impossible. E-FRAC-21 Proof 2.

STRUCTURAL (n=3,4): K4 CT length = 97 (prime). Hill cipher requires input length divisible by n. For n=3: 97/3 is not integer. For n=4: 97/4 is not integer. No padding scheme preserves the exact known crib positions at the end of the block structure. EXHAUSTIVE (n=2): hill_cipher_analysis.py tested ALL 157,248 invertible 2×2 matrices (det≠0 mod 26) in both AZ and KA alphabets, at all block offsets. Best result: 7/24 crib matches (M=[[9,7],[0,3]], offset=0), indistinguishable from noise (expected ~7.5/24 for period-2 random). Additionally, direct linear algebra from the 22 consecutive crib pairs produces an overdetermined system with no consistent 2×2 solution (verified across all block alignments). Hill + Vigenere (periods 1-4): no valid configuration found. Hill + transposition: no crib word fragments ≥6 chars found in any decrypted output. From MEMORY.md: E-AUDIT-05.

Rail Fence is a pure transposition cipher — it permutes the characters of the plaintext without changing their identities. STRUCTURAL: K4 CT has exactly 2 occurrences of the letter E. The EASTNORTHEAST crib (positions 21-33) requires the plaintext to contain the letter E at three positions (21, 30 = EASTNORTH**E**AST). BERLINCLOCK positions 63-73 = B,E,R,L,I,N,C,L,O,C,K — adds a 4th E. Since pure transposition preserves letter frequencies, no rail fence can produce a CT with only 2 E's from a PT that contains ≥3 E's. This is equivalent to the letter-frequency impossibility proof for all pure transpositions. Covered under Myszkowski/AMSCO/Rail Fence/Nihilist Transposition elimination (MEMORY.md).
eliminated.reproducibility: Structural proof — no script needed. Verified from CT letter counts.

eliminated.evidence: The Nihilist substitution cipher works by: (1) converting PT letters to 2-digit numbers (11-55) via a Polybius square, (2) adding a key (also Polybius-encoded) digit-by-digit to produce 2-4 digit numeric ciphertext. Output is always a sequence of decimal digits, not alphabetic characters. K4 CT consists entirely of 26 uppercase letters (A-Z) with zero digit characters. This is a structural impossibility — no Nihilist key, Polybius square, or digit-to-letter post-mapping scheme can resolve this without additional undisclosed encoding steps (which would constitute a separate cipher layer, not the Nihilist cipher itself). Analogous to Straddling Checkerboard / VIC elimination (E-FRAC-21 Proof 3). The Nihilist TRANSPOSITION variant is a pure transposition, eliminated by the E-frequency mismatch proof above (same as Rail Fence).
eliminated.reproducibility: Structural proof — CT has 0 digit characters, Nihilist produces digits. Verified from CT definition.

eliminated.evidence: Periods 2-8: eliminated in Session 10 via single-group algebraic analysis (crib positions within same block force contradictions). Periods 9-14: eliminated by e_s_42b_trifid_extended.py using cross-group constraint propagation and union-find. Period 9: same-cell contradictions (E=L, I=T, N=S) + pigeonhole contradiction (['E','L','N','S'] need 4 distinct values from {0,1,2}). Period 10: same-cell. Period 11: same-cell. Period 12: same-cell. Period 13: same-cell. Period 14: same-cell. Higher periods: crib positions 21-33 and 63-73 always generate sufficient cross-block constraints; the 27-letter gap (period-dividing structure) ensures contradictions persist. Additionally: Trifid output IC pattern incompatible with K4 (from E-FRAC main analysis). E-S-42b, E-S-44.

summary.key_structural_argument: K4 CT length = 97 (odd prime) eliminates ALL digraph/fractionation ciphers (Playfair, Four-Square, Two-Square, ADFGVX, ADFGX) by parity. All 26 letters present in CT eliminates 5x5-grid ciphers. Crib contradictions eliminate all monoalphabetic ciphers. Frequency mismatch (2 E's in CT vs 3+ in PT) eliminates all pure transpositions.
summary.what_remains_open: Multi-layer combinations (running key + transposition, bespoke procedural ciphers, VIC-like chart-based systems, K5 joint attack). See memory/eliminations.md and reports/final_synthesis.md.
summary.masking_caveat: Per Scheidt WIRED 2005 ('I masked the English language'), K4 plaintext may have been masked before encryption. IC-based eliminations (Bifid 6x6, simple IC tests) are weakened under this model. Structural/algebraic proofs remain valid regardless.
